body.text: Crypto News Bot — Daily Digest
July 6, 2026

Open USD (OUSD) — a new dollar stablecoin backed by 140+ firms including Stripe, Visa, Mastercard, and BlackRock — went live on Solana this week, one of the biggest challenges yet to Tether and Circle's dominance.
Stablecoin News
Stripe, Visa, Mastercard, BlackRock launch rival stablecoin Open USD
A coalition of 140+ banks and fintechs launched Open USD (OUSD) on Solana, passing reserve yield back to businesses that mint and hold the token rather than keeping it as issuer profit.
EU MiCA transitional period ends, full authorization now required

As of July 1, every Crypto-Asset Service Provider in the EU must hold full MiCA authorization or shut down; penalties reach 10% of global turnover or €10M, plus personal liability for senior managers.
GENIUS Act rulemaking deadline looms July 18
Six U.S. federal agencies tasked with finalizing stablecoin rules under the GENIUS Act have until July 18 to publish the joint regulatory framework.
Tether launches stablecoin wallet; Circle's Arc chain moves to proof-of-stake
Tether rolled out its own consumer stablecoin wallet while Circle continued transitioning its Arc chain to a proof-of-stake consensus model.
Hacking & Security Incidents
KelpDAO exploit hits $294M, among 2026's largest
Investigators describe the KelpDAO breach as a sophisticated, state-directed operation exploiting infrastructure rather than a simple smart-contract bug.
Drift Protocol breach drains roughly $285M
TRM Labs attributes the Drift Protocol loss to an infrastructure compromise; combined with KelpDAO, the two incidents account for nearly 59% of all H1 2026 crypto theft.
North Korea-linked groups tied to 66% of H1 2026 crypto theft
Crypto hacks hit record frequency in H1 2026, though total losses stayed just under $1B; DPRK-linked actors are estimated behind roughly $643M of that total.
Coordinated attack drains 100+ EVM wallets for $107K
A mysterious, automated attack swept across EVM-compatible chains, draining small amounts from over 100 wallets in a pattern researchers are still tracing.
